luca.title: 👦 Luca's Day
luca.html:
Luca gets up at seven o'clock from Monday to Friday. He eats breakfast at 7:45 and he goes to school at 8:15.

But at the weekends Luca likes to sleep. On Sunday, he gets up at 9:15 and he eats breakfast at 9:30.

At school, Luca has lunch at 12:45, but on Sunday he has his lunch at 13:30.

Luca loves Sunday!

sarah.title: 📖 Sarah's Rules
sarah.html:
After school Sarah does her homework.

Her mother says she can't use the cell phone until after dinner, but she can use the tablet and headphones to listen to music.

She can use the computer to write e-mails, but she can't play computer games.

Sarah can use her camera before dinner, and after dinner she can watch TV.

questions.question: Look, read and write the words. Complete with: in front of, above, behind, across from.
questions.sentence: The house is _____ the pond. The smoke is _____ the building. The flowers are _____ the pond. The trees are _____ the pond. The rabbit is _____ the house. The birds are _____ the big tree. The trash can is _____ the big tree.

questions.question: Write the words in the correct order: It / Is / fountain? / behind / the →
Write the words in the correct order: across from / It's / recycling plant. / the →
questions.answer: It's across from the recycling plant.

questions.question: Write the words in the correct order: the / birds / between / are / trees. / The →
questions.answer: The birds are between the trees.

questions.question: Write the words in the correct order: rabbit? / Where's / the →
Write the words in the correct order: cat / Is / a / there / in front of / table? / the →
questions.answer: Is there a cat in front of the table?

questions.question: Complete the sentences with the correct word.
questions.sentence: Please throw that bad apple in the _____. I made the fire with _____. It is important to protect our _____. We need to _____ the amount of trash in our park. There's _____ on sunny days. I always _____ plastic bags and bottles.

questions.question: Luca eats breakfast before eight o'clock.
questions.explanation: Luca eats breakfast at 7:45, which is before 8:00.

questions.question: He goes to school after he eats breakfast.
Luca gets up early on Sunday.
questions.explanation: On Sunday he gets up at 9:15, which is late.

questions.question: He has lunch at the same time every day.
questions.explanation: At school lunch is at 12:45, on Sunday at 13:30.

questions.question: He has lunch late on Sunday.
Luca's favourite day is Saturday.
questions.explanation: The text says Luca loves Sunday.

questions.question: Write the correct word from the box.
questions.sentence: Person who works in a library: _____.	Marked path to cross the road: _____.	Hitting a door: _____.	Computer you can carry: _____.	Cleaning hands with soap: _____.	Paper on the ground: _____.	Storing things neatly: _____.	Catching someone: _____.	Person who saves lives at pool: _____.	Marked path with person helping: _____.

questions.question: Sarah does her homework after dinner.
questions.explanation: Sarah does her homework after school, not after dinner.

questions.question: She can't talk with her friends before dinner.
questions.explanation: She can't use the cell phone until after dinner.

questions.question: She can listen to music quietly.
She can't write to her friends.
questions.explanation: She can use the computer to write e-mails.

questions.question: She can't play games on the computer.
She can't take photos after dinner.
questions.explanation: The text says she can use her camera before dinner — it doesn't say she can't after.

questions.question: She can watch TV before she goes to bed.
Complete the sentences with the time.
questions.sentence: Mandy goes to school at _____ (seven thirty). Tom eats lunch at _____ (twelve o'clock). She does her homework at _____ (five thirty). They play in the playground at _____ (quarter to eleven). He has a shower at _____ (eight fifteen). She goes to bed at _____ (half past nine).

questions.question: Complete the sentences with can or may.
questions.sentence: _____ I watch TV? No, you can't.	May I visit my friend? Yes, you _____.	_____ I go outside? No, you may not.	Can I have some salad? _____, you can.	Can I have some cake? _____, you can't.	May I listen to music? _____, you may not.

questions.question: Circle the correct word: The cat ran _____ from the dog.
Circle the correct word: Mike and Ann cleaned up / _____ the living room.
Circle the correct word: I fell _____ and hurt my knee.
Circle the correct word: Claire put _____ her toys.
Complete the sentences with the correct word: invite, rude, thoughtful, wash.
questions.sentence: Please _____ your hands before you eat. He was _____ and made her cry. He will _____ her to his party. She was _____ and helped me study.

questions.question: Possessive adjectives and pronouns: I / you
Possessive adjectives and pronouns: he / she
Possessive adjectives and pronouns: we / they
